cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about when the telephone booth was fitted. The options are A through D. Let me read through the story again to find the relevant part. The first paragraph mentions: "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth..." So, the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months later the booth was put up. Wait, so if it was a year ago that the line was installed, then six months after that would be 1.5 years ago? Or wait, the timeline here is that the phone line was installed a year ago. Then, six months later, the phone booth was put up. So that would be six months after the installation of the line. So the total time from when the line was installed to the booth being put up is six months. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The story says that the phone line was installed a year ago, and then six months later the booth was installed. But then the painter came after that. The man is there now, and he mentions that the minister will come next month to inaugurate it. So the process isn't complete yet.
